reviews.review_text: Cleaning and maintenance staff do what they can. Management and their presence is non existence. This park has so much potential, which islacking from new owners over the last 3 years. The should stick to their 7-11's they own and stay away from hospitality. Such a shame. Price is high for condition of amenities.
Nice 1 bedroom cabin plenty of room big TV in lounge and bedroom approximately 15 mins from city

So noisy!!!!
The biggest downside specifically of the A line units in this holiday park is the noise from the road! It honestly felt like at time our bedroom was in the emergency lane of the road. Especially when trucks passed oh my gosh it felt like they would come through the unit! We stayed once before but in a 1 bedroom unit further in the premises and I don’t remember it being noisy. So this is mainly a warning about the Aline units. The unit itself was nice and clean and beds comfy, just a shame I only got 2 hours sleep the whole night due to the road noise.
It was also cheaper booking via booking.com than direct with the holiday park, and when I emailed the holiday park about booking direct for the same price I had no response.

They might need to amend their description about a peaceful surroundings and all you hear is birds and frogs!

And the swimming pool, an absolute nice bath! I would be shocked if anyone could actually get in. It was 26deg and last year and this year my son put his foot in and screamed. Why even bother having a pool if it’s impossible to get in to

A- line cabins are quite nice and very comfortable.Setting is beautiful and location is good.Stayed for a week with not a staff member or cleaner to be seen. No housekeeping on rooms. Provided two towels for an entire seven day stay requested more towels which never arrived.
Mostly clean...for $130/night I expected it to be a bit cleaner - there was significant mould in the bathroom (could be a health risk!). Stayed in a 2BR cabin.

We got the cabin, really nice smart TV's in the bedroom and lounge area. Pool was good, because it had no restrictions. It was quite creepy going outside at night and having Skippy stare at you though. The only cons were cleanliness, it could do with a lot of dusting. And location, make sure you're driving because public transport isn't practical in Big Hill.

A great park on the outskirts of Bendigo whichoffers so much more than many of the other parks in the area. The caravan sites are situated amongst gumtrees which offer shade on hot summer days. The sites are served with their own ensuite, which although dated offer so much. The grassy sites are of good size and easy to access. The only negative is a cranky permanent who wants to chip many people. He just needs a Bex and a good lie down! Overall though an excellent park and definitely coming back!
Amazing location and facilities, the pool was a highlight for the kids, will definitely be staying here again!

Big sites
Quiet
Great spot

we got the house things did not work needs a lot of work there were fire hazards around the house the owner has no idea of what is wrong with the holiday park even after being there for 2 years will NOT be going back there
Fantastic place to stay, and will again
Reasonable prices. Good location re access to Bendigo.
Clean, tidy, nice park. Entry via 100km speed limit highway.
Nice cabin, quite clean. Lovely setting, even though close to the highway it wasn’t much of a problem. Cabin has plenty of space and is comfortable.
Online needs to be changed its not a caravan park ive phoned and there are no caravan site pics or caravans in their listing
Nice park with easy access and clean Ensuites.

Great value for money.
A little noisy in the cabins from road noise.

A-Line Holiday Park in Bendigo is a simple, no-frills place to stay. While the accommodation is functional, it’s definitely showing its age.
The cabin felt tired and in need of maintenance, the security door was a constant frustration as it kept jamming & coming off its track.
Reception wasn't open when we arrived on Friday afternoon. We called the number provided on the reception door and retrieved our key via a lock box. Reception wasn't open when we left on Sunday morning, I again called the number provided with no answer, so I dropped our key in the return drop box.
That said, if you're looking for basic accommodation without any extras, this place will do the job.
After checking on line when we got there after 5.30 he drive was told no vacancies very rudely. Checked again vacancies were available

Upon arrival we were shown the ‘Residence’, this is not the house we had booked in June 2024 after viewing your website. We were looking forward to staying in the ‘HOUSE’ which showed a lovely outdoor area to sit, outlook onto green grassed area, close to the pool & BBQ area, ensuite on one of the bathrooms and air conditioning. ||What we got in the ‘Residence’, was no air conditioner, dirty floors (not vacuumed or washed properly), dirty microwave, main oven doesn’t work, small oven had burnt food scraps on base, benches greasy, kids have drawn on the wall, found a baby bottle with contents in one of the rooms behind beside table, no light bulb in bedroom lamp, blown globes in ceiling lights, main TV didn’t work due to faulty power board, kitchen blind doesn’t go up, outdoor area appalling (rubbish lying around, no area set up to relax, garden dead & untidy), only 5 dining chairs (house sleeps 6), no dishwasher tablets, no extra garbage bags, unable to connect to the WiFi, rubbish under couch cushions.||Being members of the CFA, we found the area around the house and on the roof a huge fire risk due to dry leaves & branches and should be looked at immediately. Theres only one smoke detector and no fire blanket or fire extinguisher in the building. There’s also rats running up the walls, windows sills & roof. ||We did get clean linen & bathrooms and management rectified power board, replaced globes, gave dishwasher tabs & garbage bags, fixed WiFi (but we needed to log into a different network not listed in the information book), unable to fix air conditioner.

Cabin w/ ensuite, lovely & spacious, parking outside the cabin. Even though there was no one at the office for check in there is a number to call & they give you a code for the key safe to retrieve your cabin key. The only downside was the shower in the ensuite which hadn’t been cleaned properly, so I cleaned it.

No internet connection.  Contacted many times. No response 😕 |No internet connection.. poor connection. Aline is searching always. No internet . No internet. No internet. Too bad Service.. not considering our request. We packed our bags and bought extra data.

Our new favourite place to stay at Bendigo.

Great at atmosphere,  around the bush, has swimming pool and BBQ area.

We will go here agian

Absolutely shocking.|Hasn’t had a decent maintenance service in years.|40 degree day with no air con. Only to find that it hasn’t been serviced in years and was a fire hazard. When we rang the manager he was going to try and organise someone to come out maybe not today but maybe tomorrow until I had heard say it wasn’t good enough being 40 degrees with having an elderly person and 3 little kids, after hesitating for a moment, he finally said to let him see if he could call somebody otherwise it was just bad luck. He sent the cleaner down, who by the way was lovely, came to try and clean it but it hadn’t been serviced in so long that the whole thing just came out.|Mouse droppings on the bench top.|Tap that is leaking water every where.|Mould growing under the sinks.|Microwave so out dated with a massive crack through the door.|Maintenance junk in the cupboard.|Spiders in the bath.|Apparently the house had been cleaned within the last couple of days.|The garden and outside of house have not been cleaned. Again fire hazard.||Just disgusting. |Clearly the owners of the property don’t care. God knows what they are spending the money on as it certainly not the property.||We have paid a lot of money to stay here for 4 days and expect the house to be maintained properly. Not come in to what would be deemed as a fire hazard. ||We have come here a few times and will not be coming back again after this. Every time we come here there is always something wrong. This was the last straw.||I would not recommend staying here.

This is a fantastic find on the outskirts of Bendigo. It is quiet, serene, a haven to local wildlife and it has everything you need for a family getaway… pool, playground, soft grassy areas for a game of footy or cricket. There are communal bbq’s and so much shade for those warmer days. 😍
I haven't been but I'm going there for Christmas and my mum said it will be good but she hasn't even been 🤔
Wir haben dort im Auto geschlafen und hatte ein eigenes Bad am Platz. Es ist ein schön angelegter Platz und wir haben unsere ersten wilden Kangaroos dort gesehen

For over 12 years we have stayed in cabin 6. This year seems to be our last as we went to book for next year.when we were leaving and were told it is already booked out to a family. Now the new owners apparently  have no idea on allowing existing patrons the option to rebook when leaving if they chose to. It is a shame Paul and Dwyane decided to leave as this was the best place to stay. Our cabin had dust that thick on the fridge I could write my name. No tea towel or small broom and shovel to sweep the floor. When we stay it s for 4 nights Min so it,s important to us to keep clean. The state of the park and cabins now worry us as to the the cleanliness  of the place. No one in the office at anytime, it,s a shame this once beautiful place has gone to discrace.
Good amenities, friendly staff. Right on Calder hwy so a bit of truck noise all the time, nice park for a van and kid friendly.

1/5||Absolutely disgusting! Website severely needs updating as this place is nothing like the website portrays. No one at reception - no one at the property and the first impression on picking up keys is pure neglect - where you pick up keys is feral. Accomodation was filthy and when we contacted the owners they couldn’t care less. Emphasised I did not want to leave a bad review but got told whatever. Promised a $50 refund as we were told no refund even though the accomodation WAS NOT what we booked online. Can not express how filthy the accomodation was - told my word is true yet 2 months later no refund. Disgusting place and disgusting owners. Avoid at all costs!!

Absolutely disgusting! Website severely needs updating as this place is nothing like the website portrays. No one at reception - no one at the property and the first impression on picking up keys is pure neglect - where you pick up keys is feral. Accomodation was filthy and when we contacted the owners they couldn’t care less. Emphasised I did not want to leave a bad review but got told whatever. Promised a $50 refund as we were told no refund even though the accomodation WAS NOT what we booked online. Can not express how filthy the accomodation was - told my word is true yet 2 months later no refund. Disgusting place and disgusting owners. Avoid at all costs!!
OK, but no wifi, and only book in by phone, nice en-suite for each pitch.

A lovely stay here,, loved hearing the bird life and it was quiet and peaceful. There was highway noise but no bother. Our van site ensuite was very clean even though it was older,plus there were towels and soap provided. Caretaker was kind and accommodating and it was easy to add an extra night to our stay. The only negative was that the sites were very tight for reversing the caravan but if you are well experienced you'll get in.  Would recommend. Nearby drive to Bendigo.

For us, this park was ideal.
Lovely ensuite, quiet & leafy, lush grass site.
An interesting alternative to normal caravan parks.
Would definately use again.

We stayed in a cabin, after a few days the carpet smell became overpowering, carpet is a bad idea in this type of accomodation and should be replaced with hard flooring and rugs that can be cleaned. The facility seems to be run like an air BNB as the office wasn't staffed at all other than a mobile phone which was answered when required. It would be nice if there was access to the bike tracks to the rear without needing to pass through private land, when I did find a way a long the road I enjoyed a good ride around forested areas and reservoirs. Road noise can be bothersome towards the front of the facility. Mobile phone coverage is minimal for mobile data and camp internet was limiting if you want to upload large files. Located within a few minutes drive of shops, be careful pulling out onto the main road as it is a 100km zone. Take pegs, if you want to use the laundry and hang your clothes. Not a bad place but looking a little run down, on the maintenance of small items like door knobs, bathroom fans, carpets, security fly screens, gutter cleaning, removal of wasp nests etc.
Great value for money accommodation. Very clean.

Quiet and peaceful
There is a drive to the shops due to not having public transport out that far.
Great cabin accommodations

Sadly it lost a star & will soon lose more as all the gardens are dying due to not being watered.  I stayed twice, 3 months apart, & was shocked at the deterioration.  If things get watered I'd give it 5 stars as private sites, camp kitchen, pool area & ensuite all excellent.  My favourite park ...but for how much longer?  PS campers need to be told the C/kitchen code is on reverse of info page!
Good sized cabins with ample space around them.
Good for a home base, very close to the forest and just up the road from the shopping centre.
owners don't answer calls or messages, the pool was green slime, toilet was broken. save your money book somewhere else

Good service, but the site for our van was awkward to park on and not level. Would have more sense to put the camper van that was on a concrete block onto our grass site and our van on the gravel and concrete  block site. BBQ was good but 85% of the chairs were broken and the tables weren't clean. Camp kitchen was extremely basic and looked like no one ever used it. I'd only go here as a last choice if I was in Bendigo

First time staying here overall very impressed with this park. Had a powered Ensuite site. Tranquil shady setting enclosed by lots of trees, lawns and bush. Well designed park layout. Nice pool, BBQ area and separate camp kitchen. Nice private camping bays full of plants as private hedges, very convenient with the Ensuite being so close. Only negatives would be lack of bins Inc. recycling ones here, lights at night that come on and disrupt campers, shame about the traffic noise at times during the night it was a little bit disruptive, but not too bad overall just mostly background noise, and I thought the man at reception could have been friendlier and more helpful. Overall I would highly recommend this place to others. Seemed like a good price $48 per night and $52 fri/sat. Easy quick 15min trip into Bendigo

We had an ensuite site , reasonable price , excellent internet 🛜, nice and quiet , pretty birdsong. |Negatives , no one on site to sort out problems for the 3 nights we were there. |The site was too small so difficult to fit van onto.

We camped here for 3 nights and were not disappointed! A wonderful peaceful park, where every site has an ensuite and a private leafy space! At $47 a night powered site it was great value! The pool was welcome after a hot day exploring Bendigo. The resident kangaroos were a bonus, but wild enough not to be a nuisance. Good well equipped camp kitchen! Highly recommended. Felt like you were in the country but close to the city.

Really nice and spacious.
Very relaxing.
Highly recommend.

A-frame accommodation is at the front of the block right on the Calder Hwy which was quite noisy. There are cabins further back which would be quieter.
About 20 mins out of Bendigo CBD.
Accommodation is pretty basic, but clean and functional, beds comfortable, reverse cycle air up and down stairs, nice shower.
Tea bags and coffee/ sugar/ milk provided.
Bedding, towels and mini soaps provided, but not shampoos.
Playground for kids plus plenty of room for them to run around. Note there's a small "creek" running through.
Web photos are from a while ago- it's looking a bit rundown now, with some gardening needed, gutters due a clean out, and some general TLC.
Swimming pool is currently green.
Lots of birdlife. No real fence to the back so likely does still have kangaroos sometimes, but we didn't see them.
Also has an outdoor bbq area. Plenty of shade.
Its amazing spot. It has insuit bathrooms for each camp spot.

A line holiday park is in a good location,  on the outskirts of Bendigo,  about 8kms from the fountain.
Not a very large place, but has cabins and powered van sites are available.
Pool and camp kitchen, BBQ area.
Great for the kids to run around and play.
From here, it's just a short hop skip and a jump from all the attractions Bendigo has to offer.
All the big chain stores (Woolies/Coles, supercheap etc) are  close by, about 1.5kms heading towards Bendigo 👍

Stayed here 3 nights. We were allocated a gravel site instead of a grass one, manager answered the phone via his video doorbell and resolved the issue externally. Very good service large flat site, off the highway but protected from the noise and clean ensuites. I'll come back again.

A very well set up place to stay. Very much enjoyed the facilities both Cabin and powered ensuite. Plenty of room. Good for family and couples alike. Clean tidy. Not far from Bendigo city. Easy drive straight in.
Beautiful private rooms, we had the two bed room triangle houses and it was so beautifully done, will be back

My wife and I stayed on a powered site with an ensuite.
Beautifully laid out Park with lots of trees and well maintained.
Highway was a bit noisy but not too bad. The ensuite was basic with great water pressure, but water temperature needs to be tempered as if you're not careful, you could scold yourself. Overall, we loved our experience and would have no hesitation in returning.
